description: Stable machine-consumed trigger codes that explain why human approval is required.

codes.summary: Stage capability manifest sign-off is required before execution can proceed.
Execution requests a reduced-assurance backend such as the explicit container fallback.
A deterministic gate or policy checkpoint is being overridden by human approval.
Gateway egress enablement, allowlist changes, or allowed egress data-class expansion requires approval.
Git remote mutation operations such as push, tag update, and pull-request creation require exact final approval.
External anchor submission requires explicit signed-manifest opt-in and exact final approval on the shared gateway mutation path.
A write would occur outside the declared workspace path allowlist.
Issuing or renewing a secret lease from secretsd requires explicit human approval.
A dependency network fetch/cache-fill action via dependency-fetch gateway requires human review.
A system-modifying command outside ordinary workspace edit or test executors requires approval.
Promoting unapproved excerpts into approved excerpts requires explicit human approval artifacts.
High-risk or policy-gated project lifecycle apply operations (for example substrate initialization, upgrade, or remediation apply) must reuse the shared approval model instead of a setup-only lane.
